acquisition method: the experiment was performed using a data-dependent analysis (DDA) setting to select the “top twenty” most-abundant ions for MS/MS analysis.
alkylation reagent: alkylated in 100 mM iodoacetamide in 25 mM AB for 1 h at room temperature in the dark.
alkylation concentration: alkylated in 100 mM iodoacetamide in 25 mM AB for 1 h at room temperature in the dark.
cleavage agent: proteins were digested by the trypsin solution overnight at 37 °C.
enrichment method: we have opted for a precipitation-based protocol employing the commercially available reagent EV-GAG®.
fractionation method: we ultimately used 2D electrophoresis to visualize the protein profiles of the NORMO EVs.
instrument: LC-MS/MS analyses were performed using Q-Exactive HF-X Orbitrap mass spectrometer.
ionization type: Mass spectrometry (MALDI-ToF–ToF/MS) of selected spots identified 47 proteins.
reduction reagent: the sample was heated at 95 °C for 7 min, and total proteins were loaded and resolved on a 10% polyacrylamide gel.
reduction concentration: 100 mM DTE in 25 mM AB for 1 h at room temperature.
